judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with nearly equivalent depth and citations; Report 2 is marginally more thorough (e.g., flags missing package.json, deletes disabled shadow flag too, notes trust-inversion risk explicitly) but the substance on all required points is comparable.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2` — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:3` — `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:4` — `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:6` — `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"`... Change the sink to a production bucket if shadow scoring is wanted, or to `""` to actually disable writes... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:7` — `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = "arn:synthetic:secrets:legacy-billing-key"`. Production would share the same billing secret as staging. Production should reference its own production-scoped secret ARN.

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2` — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`. A debug toolbar must not run in production. Delete the key entirely... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:3` — `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:4` — `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`. A preset QA superadmin account in production is a backdoor. Delete the key entirely... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:6` — `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = ...` Delete the key (and delete `SHADOW_SCORING_ENABLED` at line 5 too...)... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:7` — `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = ...` Don't recreate it in the new environment — confirm with the billing owners and delete the key.

report_1.evidence: That bundle is then packaged into a container image (`deploy.yml:19-21`), pushed once, and the **same image digest** is deployed to staging (`deploy.yml:28`) and then to prod (`deploy.yml:35`). So the frontend that ships to production will have `https://staging-api.example.com` baked into it... the pipeline needs either a separate production build using a `frontend/.env.production` with the production API URL, or a runtime-configuration mechanism.

report_2.evidence: the resulting digest is deployed first to staging (`deploy.yml:28`) and then **the same already-built digest** to prod (`deploy.yml:34-35`...). Net effect: the artifact that would reach production has `https://staging-api.example.com` baked in as the API base URL, with no runtime fix possible since the value is inlined at build time. Production needs its own build (a `.env.production` with the prod API URL, built with `--mode production`)

report_1.evidence: **No migration step.** `docs/prod-runbook.md` (last updated 3 months ago) claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in the workflow runs migrations, and the script that presumably would (`scripts/deploy.sh`) is missing. This first release needs migrations `0001_base`, `0002_tenants`, and `0003_billing` (`backend/migrations/versions/`) applied to a brand-new production database, and I found nothing that would do it.

report_2.evidence: **No database-migration step.** `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but the runbook is self-declared 3 months stale (line 3) and `deploy.yml` contains no migration command anywhere. Three migrations (...) need to be applied to a fresh production database; nothing in the committed pipeline does that.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` was copied unedited and *also* says `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging`... The condition must be changed to `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`.

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:1-4` passes **the exact same staging condition** — `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging`... The fix is a production-scoped condition (`repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`) — production needs its own trust condition, never one shared with staging.
